metaDescription: Arabic calligraphy portfolio of Nadia Pasha featuring Ottoman Riq'a and Diwani inspired works, artist statement, process, and commission inquiries.
heroEyebrow: Traditional Ottoman Scripts. Contemporary Abstract Energy.
heroCopy: Explore original works created with the traditional qalam on handmade, dyed, treated, aged, and burnished paper. Each piece balances classical discipline with modern abstract expression.
statementHeading: Tradition in Dialogue With Contemporary Form
statementParagraph1: My artistic process is fueled by the challenge of creativity within the disciplined structure of Arabic and Islamic calligraphy. Holding a certificate of mastery in the Ottoman traditions of Riq'a and Diwani scripts, I work to preserve the historical integrity of each letter while shaping a fresh visual language.
statementParagraph2: Before using the qalam on paper, I develop ideas digitally to explore background, structure, and composition. I then spend hours refining letterforms and rhythm by hand. Through this process, I aim to bridge traditional artistry and contemporary innovation, inviting viewers into a visual dialogue that transcends time and culture.
exhibitions: Diploma to teach Riq'a and Diwani, Turkiye
Two decades volunteering in the Art Literacy program, Beaverton School District
Practice rooted in Ottoman calligraphic traditions
commissionText: Original Arabic calligraphy artworks, custom calligraphic compositions, and abstract-script pieces for private and public spaces.
aboutBio: Art is my passion, and I have always found ways to fuel creativity. I grew up in Saudi Arabia and Pakistan, where rich artistic cultures laid the foundation for my voice as an artist. While abstract art has long been central to my expression, the aesthetic precision of calligraphy drew me deeply. After years of independent study, I pursued formal training in Arabic calligraphy and traveled to Turkiye to complete my diploma in Riq'a and Diwani. I work with traditional tools, infusing ancient scripts with a modern abstract twist.
processHeading: From Concept Sketch to Qalam on Paper
processIntro: Each artwork is developed through iterative composition studies before final hand lettering on prepared paper.

processSteps.title: Digital Composition Study
processSteps.description: Initial structure and letter rhythm are explored in Procreate before committing ink to paper.

processSteps.title: Qalam and Ink Drafting
processSteps.description: Traditional reed pen control establishes weight, flow, and spacing in the script.

processSteps.title: Final Burnished Presentation
processSteps.description: Finished compositions are refined for exhibition-ready framing and archival display.
